Workshop.title: Workshop
Workshop.description: Represents a mechanic workshop registered in the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the workshop entity.

description.description: Detailed description of the workshop and its services.

latitude.description: Latitude coordinate of the workshop's location.

longitude.description: Longitude coordinate of the workshop's location.

obdScannerService.description: Indicates whether the workshop offers OBDII scanner services.

serviceIds.description: References to Services offered by the workshop. (Relationship: Workshop 1:N Service)

ownerId.description: The UID of the user who owns this workshop.

averageRating.description: The average rating of the workshop, from 1 to 5.

reviewCount.description: The total number of reviews for the workshop.

Appointment.title: Appointment
Appointment.description: Represents a customer appointment at a workshop.

id.description: Unique identifier for the appointment entity.

workshopId.description: Reference to the Workshop. (Relationship: Workshop 1:N Appointment)

userId.description: Reference to the User. (Relationship: User 1:N Appointment)

appointmentDateTime.description: Date and time of the appointment.

description.description: Customer provided notes regarding the appointment.

status.description: Status of the appointment (e.g., scheduled, completed, cancelled).

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

Service.title: Service
Service.description: Represents a service offered by a workshop.

id.description: Unique identifier for the service entity.

name.description: Name of the service (e.g., Oil Change, Tire Rotation, OBDII Scan).

Review.title: Review
Review.description: Represents a customer review for a workshop.

id.description: Unique identifier for the review entity.

workshopId.description: Reference to the Workshop. (Relationship: Workshop 1:N Review)

userId.description: Reference to the User who wrote the review.

comment.description: The text content of the review.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the review was created.

definition.description: Stores details of mechanic workshops. Includes denormalized 'obdScannerService' boolean to enable secure filtering for workshops offering OBDII services.

params.name: workshopId
params.description: The unique identifier for the workshop.

definition.description: Stores customer reviews for a specific workshop.

params.name: workshopId
params.description: The unique identifier for the workshop.

params.name: reviewId
params.description: The unique identifier for the review.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Each user has a unique ID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores appointment data for the related User.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: appointmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the appointment.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the service.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the MechConnect application, focusing on mechanic workshop registration, appointment booking, and OBDII scanner service highlighting. The structure prioritizes authorization independence and supports QAPs (Rules are not Filters) to ensure secure `list` operations. Path-based ownership is used where applicable to simplify security rules. The structure is organized into collections for `Workshops`, `Users`, `Services`, and `Appointments` with subcollections to manage relationships effectively.

**Authorization Independence:**

*   **Appointments:** The `appointments` subcollection under each `user` document includes the `userId` of the user who created the appointment. This denormalization is critical for authorization independence because security rules can authorize access to appointments based on the `userId` in the appointment document and/or ownership of the parent `User` document. Without the `userId` on the `Appointment`, rules would need to perform a `get()` to verify the requesting user owns the parent `User` document, violating Authorization Independence.
*   **Reviews**: Reviews are stored in a subcollection under each workshop (`/workshops/{workshopId}/reviews`). This allows for easy retrieval of all reviews for a specific workshop and enables security rules that grant write access only to authenticated users.

**QAPs Support:**

*   **Workshops Collection:** All workshops are stored in a single `workshops` collection, but segregation is achieved via the `obdScannerService` field. List operations can be secured by filtering based on workshop attributes in the rules (e.g., `obdScannerService == true`) to highlight workshops offering OBDII scanner services, without compromising security.
*   **User-Owned Appointments:** Storing appointments within a user's document ensures that only the user has access to their appointment data.

This design ensures that security rules can be written to validate the existence of a workshop before an appointment or review can be created, linking the User, Workshop, and Appointment/Review documents together in a manageable and scalable way.
